_ai: AI fills this. Derived from: architecture.json + non-functional-requirements.json + product.json (pricing tiers). Do not edit manually.

infrastructure.regionRationale: Primary customer base is European (EU GDPR jurisdiction). eu-west-1 satisfies data residency requirements, minimises latency for UK and mainland Europe, and is within the same AWS region group as eu-central-1 (Frankfurt) used for cross-region backup replication.

backend.framework: Spring Boot 3.x (one Spring Boot application per bounded context service; multi-module Gradle monorepo)
backend.buildTool: Gradle 8 (multi-module monorepo; shared buildSrc conventions; Gradle wrapper committed to repo)
backend.containerisation: Docker (multi-stage builds; non-root user; eclipse-temurin:25-jre-alpine base image for production)
backend.orchestration: AWS ECS Fargate (serverless containers — no EC2 node management; auto-scaling per service via Application Auto Scaling on CPU/queue depth)
backend.serviceDiscovery: AWS Cloud Map (private DNS namespaces per environment) + AWS Application Load Balancer (path-based routing to ECS services)
backend.databaseMigrations: Flyway (versioned, forward-only migrations; run as ECS one-off task in deployment pipeline before service rollout)
backend.connectionPooling: PgBouncer (transaction-mode pooling; deployed as ECS sidecar per data-plane service)

frontend.framework: Next.js 14 (React Server Components for booking pages; client components for interactive slot browser)
frontend.hosting: AWS Amplify Hosting (CI/CD integration; server-side rendering on Lambda@Edge; Git-based deployments)
frontend.cdn: Amazon CloudFront (static assets; booking page HTML cached at edge with tenant_id-aware cache keys)
frontend.tenantRouting: Subdomain routing: {tenant_slug}.anji-schedulo.com for Starter and Pro. Custom domain (CNAME to CloudFront) for Enterprise with SSL via AWS Certificate Manager (product.json Enterprise extras).
frontend.stateManagement: Zustand (lightweight client state for booking wizard steps)

starterPro.instanceType: db.t4g.medium (shared cluster for Starter and Pro tenants — ADR002)
starterPro.storageType: gp3 SSD, 100 GB initial with autoscaling to 1 TB
starterPro.connectionPooler: PgBouncer (transaction mode, max 200 connections per service)
starterPro.rlsEnforcement: SET LOCAL app.tenant_id at transaction start; RLS policies on all tables; safe default returns zero rows when context is null (BR005, ADR002)

enterprise.instanceType: db.r7g.large (dedicated cluster per Enterprise tenant — product.json Enterprise extras)
enterprise.rlsEnforcement: Same RLS policies as shared cluster for defence in depth (BR005)

database.backupStrategy: AWS RDS automated daily snapshots (retained 7 days). Point-in-time recovery (PITR) with WAL archiving to S3 — RPO: 5 minutes. Manual snapshot taken before every schema migration. Cross-region snapshot copy to eu-central-1 (Frankfurt) nightly.
database.encryptionAtRest: AES-256 via AWS KMS (aws/rds managed key for Starter/Pro; customer-managed KMS key for Enterprise)
database.encryptionInTransit: TLS 1.3 enforced (ssl=require in all connection strings; RDS parameter group ssl_min_protocol_version=TLSv1.2)

cache.instanceType: cache.t4g.medium (single-shard cluster mode disabled; single AZ with read replica in alternate AZ)

useCases.ttl: 30 seconds — short TTL to balance read performance with freshness (NFR005)

cache.encryptionInTransit: TLS 1.3 (in-transit encryption enabled on ElastiCache cluster)

messageBus.technology: AWS SNS (fan-out) + AWS SQS FIFO (per-consumer delivery)
messageBus.topology: One SNS topic per event type (e.g. appointment-confirmed-topic). Each consumer service has its own SQS FIFO queue per subscribed topic (e.g. notification-service-appointment-confirmed-queue). Subscriptions use SNS-to-SQS fan-out with filter policies where needed.
messageBus.ordering: SQS FIFO with MessageGroupId = tenant_id guarantees per-tenant ordering without blocking other tenants (NFR011, ADR003)
messageBus.dlqConfiguration: maxReceiveCount = 4 on all source queues. DLQ named {source-queue-name}-dlq. DLQ retention: 14 days.
messageBus.retentionPeriod: 14 days on all queues (satisfies NFR003 — messages durable during temporary consumer downtime)
messageBus.encryptionAtRest: AWS KMS encryption enabled on all SNS topics and SQS queues (NFR013)

buckets.name: anji-schedulo-tenant-exports-{env}
buckets.purpose: Tenant data export archives (FR012). Contains NDJSON archives of all tenant data scoped per tenant.
buckets.lifecyclePolicy: Expire objects 90 days after creation (BR012). Objects are also deleted via signed URL expiry (24 hours for download links, FR012).
buckets.access: Private; presigned URLs generated by ops-service with 24-hour expiry

buckets.name: anji-schedulo-event-archives-{env}
buckets.purpose: Monthly exports of appointment_events table for long-term event log durability and disaster recovery. Source for replay if RDS is unavailable.
buckets.lifecyclePolicy: Standard storage for 1 year; transition to S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval after 1 year; retain payment-related events for 7 years (BR012).
buckets.access: Private; accessible to ops-service and outbox-relay only

buckets.name: anji-schedulo-audit-logs-{env}
buckets.purpose: Immutable export of AuditLog table for permanent retention (BR012, BR014). Exported nightly.
buckets.lifecyclePolicy: Object Lock (WORM mode) — no deletion. Indefinite retention.
buckets.access: Private; write-only from audit export job; read by ops-service for compliance queries

secrets.name: anji-schedulo/{env}/db/password
secrets.contents: PostgreSQL master password and application user passwords
secrets.rotationPeriod: 30 days (automatic rotation via Secrets Manager Lambda rotation function)
secrets.injectionMethod: ECS task definition secrets injection (ARN reference; injected as environment variables at container start)

secrets.name: anji-schedulo/{env}/stripe/secret-key
secrets.rotationPeriod: 90 days (manual rotation; Stripe key rotation workflow documented in ops runbook)

secrets.name: anji-schedulo/{env}/jwt/private-key
secrets.contents: RS256 private key for JWT signing (api-gateway)
secrets.rotationPeriod: 90 days (manual rotation with rolling key support — new key published, old key still accepted for 15-minute grace period)

secrets.name: anji-schedulo/{env}/jwt/public-key
secrets.contents: RS256 public key for JWT validation (all services that validate tokens directly)

secrets.name: anji-schedulo/{env}/sendgrid/api-key
secrets.rotationPeriod: 180 days (manual — SendGrid does not support automated rotation)

secrets.name: anji-schedulo/{env}/twilio/auth-token
secrets.contents: Twilio Account SID and Auth Token (notification-service, Pro and Enterprise only)

secrets.name: anji-schedulo/{env}/redis/auth-token
secrets.rotationPeriod: 30 days (automatic via Secrets Manager + ElastiCache token rotation)

useCases: Booking velocity spike detection (sliding 5-minute window aggregation per tenant, FR009)
Cancellation rate monitoring (rolling 50-booking window per tenant, FR009)
streamProcessing.consumerService: analytics-service (Enhanced Fan-Out consumer for low-latency processing)
streamProcessing.note: Kinesis is in scope from v1.0. MVP analytics-service reads directly from SQS without Kinesis. Kinesis is introduced when sub-second anomaly detection is required.

pipelineSteps: Integration tests (real PostgreSQL via Testcontainers; cross-tenant isolation tests — NFR012)
Run Flyway migrations (ECS one-off task against target environment)
Deploy ECS services (rolling update, 50% minimum healthy, 200% maximum)
Rollback on smoke test failure (previous task definition reactivated)
cicd.branchStrategy: main → production; release/* → staging; feature/* → dev on PR
